"cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-20", "name": "Improper Input Validation"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-611", "name": "Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-918", "name": "Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)"}], "description": "### Summary
A GeoServer that uses `ENTITY_RESOLUTION_ALLOWLIST` may allow attacker to perform unauthenticated Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF).

### Details
This vulnerability requires that GeoServer is set up to use a proxy base URL and the `ENTITY_RESOLUTION_ALLOWLIST` (default since 2.25.0):

### Impact
This vulnerability allows an attacker to cause GeoServer to make requests to an unintended location.

### Workaround
GeoServer installations are only affected by this vulnerability if they use a proxy base URL that does not contain a URL path or end with a slash (e.g., `https://somesite.org` instead of `https://somesite.org/` or `https://somesite.org/geoserver`). If the proxy base URL does not contain a path, adding a slash to the end of the URL will mitigate this vulnerability.

### Resources
https://osgeo-org.atlassian.net/browse/GEOS-11867
https://github.com/geoserver/geoserver/pull/8622

### Credits:
-  Le Mau Anh Phong at Verichains Cyber Force", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00287, "percentile": 0.20527}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-x4r9-gmw3-hxww", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-12T18:23:35Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x4r9-gmw3-hxww", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-x4r9-gmw3-hxww"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2025-58175"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-12T18:23:35Z", "references": ["https://github.com/geoserver/geoserver/security/advisories/GHSA-x4r9-gmw3-hxww", "https://github.com/geoserver/geoserver/pull/8622", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x4r9-gmw3-hxww"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/geoserver/geoserver/security-advisories/GHSA-x4r9-gmw3-hxww", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/geoserver/geoserver", "summary": "GeoServer has a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability in its XML Entity Resolution", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-12T18:23:37Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-x4r9-gmw3-hxww", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.26.4", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "org.geoserver.web:gs-web-app"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 2.26.3"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.26.4", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "org.geoserver:gs-main"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 2.26.3"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.27.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "org.geoserver:gs-main"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.27.0, <= 2.27.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.27.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "org.geoserver.web:gs-web-app"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.27.0, <= 2.27.2"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: GeoServer has a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability in its XML Entity Resolution
